name: Gaga's Blueberry Cake
recipeInstructions:
For the cake, sift together the flour, baking powder, and salt. Cream the shortening until light and fluffy. Gradually add in the sugar, then alternate adding the dry ingredients with milk, stirring only enough to blend.
Wash the berries and lightly coat them with flour. Gently fold the floured berries into the cake batter. Pour into a greased 8x8 pan and bake at 375°F for 25-35 minutes or until a tester inserted comes out clean.
For the sauce, combine the blueberries, lemon juice, and sugar in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Cook until boiling, then reduce heat to maintain a simmer. Cook until the berries are just tender.
Serve the cake with a small amount of hard sauce on top and pour the warm sauce over the cake. For the hard sauce, combine about 1/4 cup butter with confectioners' sugar. Beat together, adding more sugar until firm but not hard, and add a dash of vanilla.
This recipe was one I translated from my Great Grandmother's Receipt book. It is a family favorite, best served warm, and enjoyed during blueberry season. The hard sauce should be at room temperature, and the blueberry sauce hot enough to melt some of the hard sauce when poured over the cake.
